skill.name: context-manager
skill.description: Elite AI context engineering specialist for dynamic context management, vector databases, knowledge graphs, and intelligent memory systems. Orchestrates context across multi-agent workflows and enterprise AI systems.
skill.summary: Expert AI skill for building dynamic context management systems, vector databases, knowledge graphs, and intelligent memory architectures.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only instructional text for AI context engineering. No executable code, network requests, file system access, or command execution patterns detected. Static analysis found 0 files with 0 lines of executable code. Risk score is 0/100 as this skill provides guidance text only, not operational code.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers design and implement dynamic context management systems for AI applications, including vector databases, knowledge graphs, and intelligent memory architectures that provide the right information to AI systems at the right time.
actual_capabilities: Design dynamic context assembly and intelligent information retrieval systems
Implement vector database solutions with Pinecone, Weaviate, or Qdrant
Build knowledge graphs with entity linking and semantic reasoning
Create intelligent memory architectures for long-running AI conversations
Optimize RAG performance for large-scale document retrieval
Orchestrate multi-agent workflows with context handoff protocols
limitations: Does not execute code or interact with external systems directly
Does not provide pre-built database infrastructure or hosting
Requires integration with specific vector databases and tools for implementation
Does not include authentication or access control implementations

use_cases.title: Enterprise Knowledge Base System
use_cases.description: Design a scalable context management system for enterprise document search using vector embeddings and semantic retrieval.
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise AI engineers building knowledge management systems

use_cases.title: Multi-Agent Customer Support Platform
use_cases.description: Create context orchestration for multi-agent customer support with intelligent handoff and state management.
use_cases.target_user: Platform developers building AI customer service solutions

use_cases.title: Long-Conversation Memory System
use_cases.description: Implement intelligent memory management for sustained AI conversations with episodic and semantic memory layers.
use_cases.target_user: AI application developers creating conversational AI products

prompt_templates.title: Basic Context System Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a context management system for [USE_CASE]. Include components for context assembly, retrieval, and optimization.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new context engineering project

prompt_templates.title: Vector Database Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me implement a vector database solution using [DATABASE_NAME] for [APPLICATION_TYPE]. Include schema design, embedding strategy, and query optimization.
prompt_templates.scenario: Implementing semantic search with vector databases

prompt_templates.title: Knowledge Graph Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a knowledge graph architecture for [DOMAIN] with entity relationships, ontology design, and query optimization strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Agent Context Orchestration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a context handoff protocol for [MULTI_AGENT_SCENARIO] including agent-specific context preparation, state management, and error recovery.

output_examples.input: Design a context management system for a customer support chatbot
output_examples.output: A comprehensive system design including: (1) Context layers: working memory for active conversation, episodic memory for history, semantic memory for knowledge base. (2) Retrieval strategy: hybrid search combining vector similarity with keyword matching. (3) Context optimization: token budget management, relevance filtering, staleness detection. (4) Agent coordination: handoff protocols, shared context contracts, state synchronization.

output_examples.input: Optimize RAG performance for 1 million documents
output_examples.output: Performance optimization strategy covering: (1) Indexing: hierarchical navigable small world (HNSW) indexes with appropriate ef_construction values. (2) Query: hybrid retrieval combining dense embeddings with sparse BM25. (3) Chunking: semantic chunking with 20% overlap for context preservation. (4) Caching: LRU cache for frequent queries, pre-computed embeddings for top queries. (5) Scaling: sharding strategy by document namespace, read replicas for query load.

best_practices: Apply tiered context strategies: keep critical info in system prompt, use RAG for secondary information, externalize large knowledge bases
Implement context versioning and change tracking to understand how context evolves over time
Use hybrid search combining vector similarity with keyword matching for more accurate retrieval
anti_patterns: Dumping all available context without prioritization - leads to token limits and reduced relevance
Ignoring context staleness - serving outdated information degrades user trust
Over-engineering before understanding actual retrieval needs - start simple, measure, then optimize

faq.question: What is context management in AI?
faq.answer: Context management is the practice of providing the right information, tools, and memory to AI systems at the right time. It includes techniques for organizing, retrieving, and optimizing information that AI models use to generate responses.

faq.question: Which vector database should I use?
faq.answer: Choose based on your needs: Pinecone for managed simplicity, Weaviate for flexibility and GraphQL, Qdrant for performance-critical applications, or Chroma for local development and prototyping.

faq.question: How does RAG improve AI responses?
faq.answer: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) improves AI responses by retrieving relevant information from external knowledge bases and including it in the model context. This allows AI to access up-to-date information beyond its training data.

faq.question: What is a knowledge graph?
faq.answer: A knowledge graph represents information as a network of entities (nodes) and their relationships (edges). It enables semantic reasoning, contextual queries, and interconnected data exploration beyond simple keyword matching.

faq.question: How do multi-agent systems share context?
faq.answer: Multi-agent systems share context through handoff protocols that transfer relevant conversation state, shared memory stores, or centralized context coordinators that prepare agent-specific context views.

faq.question: What is context window optimization?
faq.answer: Context window optimization involves strategically selecting and compressing information to fit within AI model token limits while maximizing the relevance and usefulness of the included context.
